places.name: Catacombes de Paris
places.description: The vast, legendary catacombs hold secrets much stranger than stacked bones.

places.name: Père Lachaise Cemetery
places.description: France's most famous cemetery, with some of its most curious tombs.

places.name: Bibliothèque nationale de France (National Library of France)
places.description: This massive library holds what was once the largest book collection in the world.

places.name: 59 Rivoli
places.description: Notorious artist squat renovated into legal studios.

places.name: Petite Ceinture
places.description: Abandoned railway line circling the city of Paris.

places.name: Montmartre Cemetery
places.description: A horde of stray cats rule in this secluded Parisian cemetery under a bridge.

places.name: Shakespeare and Company
places.description: This iconic Parisian bookstore doubles as the "Tumbleweed Hotel" for traveling writers.

places.name: Au Roi de la Bière
places.description: Once a 19th-century brasserie, this McDonald's exterior celebrates Alsatian design and the "King of Beer."

places.name: Roger La Grenouille
places.description: This frog-themed restaurant was frequented by Picasso, Saint-Exupéry, and more.
